questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Hardly had we left ____ it started raining.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: Earthquakes occur when tectonic plates shift suddenly. Q: The point of origin is called the epicentre. R: Seismic waves travel through the Earth's crust. S: The Richter scale measures the magnitude of earthquakes.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The manager asked ____ the report was ready.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: The automobile industry is shifting towards electric vehicles. Q: Battery technology has improved significantly in recent years. R: Charging infrastructure is being expanded rapidly. S: This transition will reduce carbon emissions.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: He prefers coffee ____ tea in the morning.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: Cyber security is a growing concern for organisations. Q: Data breaches can result in significant financial losses. R: Companies invest in firewalls and encryption technologies. S: Employee training is essential to prevent phishing attacks.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: He is ____ intelligent that he solves problems instantly.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: Shakespeare is widely regarded as the greatest writer in English. Q: He wrote 37 plays and 154 sonnets. R: His works explore universal themes of love, power, and betrayal. S: His influence on the English language is immeasurable.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The company faced losses ____ poor management decisions.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: The human brain is a complex organ. Q: It contains billions of neurons that transmit signals. R: Different regions control different functions. S: Neuroplasticity allows the brain to adapt and reorganise.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: He was prevented ____ attending the meeting.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: The internet has revolutionised communication. Q: People can now connect instantly across the globe. R: Social media platforms enable sharing of information. S: However, digital divide remains a concern.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: She is married ____ a mining engineer.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: The Earth's atmosphere is composed of several layers. Q: The troposphere is the lowest layer where weather occurs. R: Above it lies the stratosphere containing the ozone layer. S: The mesosphere and thermosphere extend further upward.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: ____ you work hard, you will not succeed.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: Climate change is one of the biggest challenges today. Q: Rising temperatures cause extreme weather events. R: Governments worldwide are taking mitigation measures. S: However, collective action at individual level is also needed.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: He is ____ a nice person that everyone likes him.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: She was angry on her friend for lying.
options: She was angry to her friend for lying.
She was angry on her friend for lying.
She was angry with her friend for lying.
She was angry at her friend for lying.
questions.solution: She was angry with her friend for lying.

questions.stem: Meaning of 'To be a sitting duck':

questions.stem: Correct: The price of these goods have increased.
options: The price of these goods has increased.
The price of these goods are increased.
The prices of these goods has increased.
The price of these goods have increased.
questions.solution: The price of these goods has increased.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'To be up in arms':

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: He is better than any student in the class.
options: He is best than any student in the class.
He is better than any other student in the class.
He is the best than any student in the class.
He is better than any student in the class.
questions.solution: He is better than any other student in the class.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of EMANCIPATE:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which statement about Little's law is correct?
options: It holds for any stable queuing system regardless of distribution
It applies only to single-server queues
questions.solution: L = λW is distribution-free; it applies to any stable system in steady state.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: 3PL (Third Party Logistics) provider offers:
questions.solution: 3PL: warehousing, transportation, fulfillment. Allows focus on core competencies. Variable cost vs fixed.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Multivariate SPC — Hotelling T²:
options: Monitor multiple correlated variables simultaneously. χ² / F distribution for UCL. Phase I vs Phase II
questions.solution: Hotelling T²: T² = n(x̄-μ₀)'Σ⁻¹(x̄-μ₀). Special cause decomposition. MEWMA, MCUSUM extensions.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: On the break-even chart shown (fixed cost ₹10000, price ₹100, variable cost ₹60/unit), the marked point where revenue meets total cost occurs at:
questions.solution: BEP = FC/(p − v) = 10000/(100 − 60) = 250 units, where total revenue = total cost.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Hurdle rate in capital budgeting is:
questions.solution: Hurdle rate: minimum return required for project approval. Reflects cost of capital and risk. Projects with IRR > hurdle accepted.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Weighted moving average: weights must sum to 1.
questions.solution: WMA: weights sum to 1. More weight to recent observations. Sum > 1 gives biased estimate.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: CORELAP (Computerized Relationship Layout Planning):
options: Construction algorithm. TCR (total closeness rating). Place highest TCR first. Relationship chart based
questions.solution: CORELAP: uses relationship chart. TCR = sum of closeness ratings. Placement: next to related depts.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Workplace ergonomics: adjustable workstation:
options: Adjustable height desk, chair, monitor arm to accommodate 5th-95th percentile users
questions.solution: Adjustable: accommodate population range. Keyboard tray, footrest, monitor riser.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Gemba walk means:
options: Going to the actual place where work is done
questions.solution: Gemba: Japanese for actual place. Leaders observe, ask questions, understand processes.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: TRIZ: contradiction matrix helps resolve:
options: Technical contradictions (improve one parameter, worsens another)
questions.solution: TRIZ: 40 inventive principles. Matrix suggests principles for specific parameter contradictions. Systematic innovation.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Mean Time Between Failure (MTBF):
options: Total operating time / number of failures. Reliability = e^(-t/MTBF)
questions.solution: MTBF: for repairable systems. MTTR: mean time to repair. Availability = MTBF/(MTBF+MTTR).

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Dispatching rules: SPT (Shortest Processing Time) minimizes:
questions.solution: SPT: shortest job first. Minimizes mean flow time, mean WIP, mean waiting time. Poor for maximum tardiness.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Postponement (delayed differentiation):
options: Generic product held longer. Customization late in supply chain
questions.solution: Postponement: Hewlett-Packard printers. Reduced inventory risk. Mass customization.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Two components of reliability 0.9 and 0.8 are in series. The system reliability is:

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: TRIZ 40 inventive principles include:
questions.solution: TRIZ principles: 40 ways to resolve contradictions.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fixed cost ₹20000, price ₹100, variable cost ₹60/unit. The output needed for a target profit of ₹10000 is:

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: WACC (Weighted Average Cost of Capital):
options: Blend of debt and equity costs weighted by proportions
questions.solution: WACC = wd×kd×(1-t) + we×ke. Weighted average reflects financing mix. Used as discount rate for projects.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Time study — number of cycles:
questions.solution: Sample size for desired accuracy. ±5% at 95% confidence. Preliminary sample of 10 cycles.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Greedy algorithm for matroid: produces:
questions.solution: Matroid: greedy gives optimal for any linear objective. Examples: MST (Kruskal), scheduling. Key property.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The control limits on a Shewhart control chart are usually set at:
options: ±3 standard deviations from the centre line
questions.solution: 3σ limits give ~99.7% of points inside when the process is in control (low false alarms).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Cognitive ergonomics:
options: Mental workload, situation awareness, decision-making. Human error taxonomy: slips, lapses, mistakes, violations
questions.solution: Cognitive: Rasmussen's SRK (skill, rule, knowledge). Reason's Swiss cheese model. HFACS.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Integer programming — branch and bound:
options: LP relaxation → branch on fractional variable → prune by bound, infeasibility, optimality
questions.solution: Branch & bound: tree search. Upper bound from LP relaxation. Lower bound from feasible IP solution.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Life cycle cost: operating cost typically exceeds initial cost by factor of:
questions.solution: Operating: 60-70% of LCC. Initial: 20-30%. Disposal: 5-10%. VE targets operating cost.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Throughput of a manufacturing cell depends on:
questions.solution: Cell throughput = capacity of bottleneck. Theory of Constraints. All work up to bottleneck pace.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An assembly line has total task time 40 min, 6 stations and a cycle time of 10 min. Its balance efficiency is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Material handling equipment:
questions.solution: Material handling: 20-50% of production cost. Unit load principle. Standardization.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Global supply chain — total landed cost:
options: Purchase price + freight + duty + insurance + inventory carrying + risk. Nearshoring calculation
questions.solution: TLC: used for sourcing decisions. Soft factors: IP risk, geopolitical stability.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Time study is used to establish the:
questions.solution: Time study (stopwatch) determines how long a qualified worker should take at a defined pace.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The last three demands were 140, 150 and 120 units. The 3-period moving-average forecast is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Dynamic programming:
options: Sequential decision. Bellman's principle of optimality. Stage, state, decision. Optimal substructure
questions.solution: DP: shortest path, inventory, resource allocation. Forward vs backward recursion. Curse of dimensionality.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Cross-docking eliminates:
questions.solution: Cross-dock: direct transfer inbound to outbound.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: VE — creativity phase tools:
options: Brainstorming, TRIZ, SCAMPER, morphological analysis, analogy
questions.solution: TEAM: 5-8 people, diverse backgrounds. Defer judgment. Quantity over quality. Combine ideas.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Throughput Accounting: operating expense is:
options: Money spent to convert inventory into throughput
questions.solution: OE = all money to run (labor, utilities, depreciation, admin). Goal: minimize OE while maximizing T.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Productivity definition:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: ALDEP (Automated Layout Design Program):
options: Constructive algorithm. Placement by relationship chart (A, E, I, O, U, X)
questions.solution: ALDEP: sweep width parameter. Scans relationship chart. Places departments in order.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fixed position layout:
options: Product stays in place, resources move (shipbuilding, aircraft, construction)
questions.solution: Fixed: large/heavy product. Cranes, tools, workers brought to site.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Warehouse — forward pick area:
options: Small quantity, fast movers. Picked for individual orders. Replenished from reserve
questions.solution: Forward: flow rack, carton flow. Reserve: pallet rack. Replenishment from reserve to forward.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Production Flow Analysis (PFA):
options: Machine-component matrix. Rearrange into cells. Rank order clustering (ROC). Bond energy algorithm
questions.solution: PFA: Burbidge. Use incidence matrix. ROC: binary rank. King's algorithm: sort rows/cols by binary value.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Production smoothing (Heijunka):
options: Level production by volume and mix. Build schedule in small batches. Reduces unevenness
questions.solution: Heijunka: box (matrix schedule). Kanban post. Level by product family. Sequence mixed models.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Effective capacity is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Linear programming is a technique to optimize a linear objective function subject to:
questions.solution: LP maximizes/minimizes a linear objective under a set of linear equality/inequality constraints.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: PMTS (Predetermined Motion Time Systems):
options: MTM (Methods-Time Measurement), MOST (Maynard Operation Sequence Technique)
questions.solution: MTM: micro-motions in TMU (1 TMU = 0.036 sec). MOST: sequence models for higher-level activities.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fixed cost = ₹50000, price = ₹100/unit, variable cost = ₹30/unit. The break-even quantity is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Ishikawa (fishbone) diagram:
options: Cause-and-effect. 6M: Man, Machine, Material, Method, Measurement, Mother Nature
questions.solution: Fishbone: brainstorms root causes. Categorize by 6M. Drill down to find root cause.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Statistical maintenance — Weibull MLE:
options: Maximum likelihood estimation of β, η. Better than probability plot. Interval censored data handling
questions.solution: MLE: iterative solution (Newton-Raphson). Suspension (right-censored) data included.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Material handling cost = sum of (flow * distance) between departments:
questions.solution: Material handling cost: C = sum(f_ij * d_ij). Objective in layout optimization.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE):
questions.solution: MAPE: Σ(|e_t|/D_t)×100/n. Percentage error. Problem when actual=0.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Safety stock: z=1.645, σ_L=20. SS=

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Daily demand = 80, lead time = 6 days, safety stock = 100 units. The reorder point is:
questions.solution: ROP = (demand × lead time) + safety stock = 80×6 + 100 = 580 units.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which statement comparing PERT and CPM is correct?
options: CPM is probabilistic; PERT is deterministic
PERT is probabilistic; CPM is deterministic
questions.solution: PERT uses three time estimates (probabilistic); CPM uses single deterministic times.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Follow-up (expediting):
options: Tracking progress, identifying delays, corrective action
questions.solution: Follow-up: ensure delivery on time. Gantt chart progress bars. Hot list for priorities.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: LP — Big M method:
options: Artificial variables with large cost M. Minimizes M×sum of artificials
questions.solution: Big M: M >> other costs. Penalty for artificials in basis. M positive for min.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A line makes 600 units using 50 labour-hours. The labour productivity is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fixed cost = ₹20000, price = ₹80/unit, variable cost = ₹60/unit. The break-even quantity is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A PERT activity has to = 2, tm = 6, tp = 12 days. Its expected time is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: NPV: Initial cost=$50000, annual benefit=$15000, r=12%, n=5 years. NPV≈

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For the CPM network shown (path 1-2-4 = A+B = 4+6, path 1-3-4 = C+D = 2+5), the project duration (critical path) is:
questions.solution: Path durations: 1-2-4 = 10, 1-3-4 = 7. The critical path is the longest = 10 days.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: On the break-even chart shown (fixed cost ₹20000, price ₹100, variable cost ₹40/unit), the marked point where revenue meets total cost occurs at:
questions.solution: BEP = FC/(p − v) = 20000/(100 − 40) = 333.3 units, where total revenue = total cost.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Risk pooling:
options: Aggregating inventory in fewer locations reduces safety stock (√N rule)
questions.solution: Risk pooling: safety stock ∝ √(demand variance). Centralization benefit: SS_central/SS_decentral = 1/√N.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Series reliability: R₁=0.8, R₂=0.9. R_series=

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Seasonal index: ratio of actual to trend value for each season. Average of indices =
questions.solution: Seasonal: SI_t = Actual_t / Trend_t. Average SI = 1.0. Used to deseasonalize or reseasonalize.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A product is used at 80 units/day with a lead time of 7 days. The reorder point (no safety stock) is:
questions.solution: ROP = demand rate × lead time = 80×7 = 560 units.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Supply chain resilience:
options: Absorptive capacity (buffer) + adaptive capacity (redirect) + restorative capacity (recover). Black swan events
questions.solution: Resilience: dual sourcing, flexible supply base, capacity buffers. Post-COVID focus.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Daily demand = 60, lead time = 4 days, safety stock = 50 units. The reorder point is:
questions.solution: ROP = (demand × lead time) + safety stock = 60×4 + 50 = 290 units.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a linear programming problem, the set of all points satisfying the constraints is the:
questions.solution: The feasible region is the (convex) set of all solutions meeting every constraint.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In exponential smoothing, a larger smoothing constant α makes the forecast:
questions.solution: Higher α weights the latest actual more, tracking changes faster (but noisier).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For the CPM network shown (path 1-2-4 = A+B = 4+8, path 1-3-4 = C+D = 2+9), the project duration (critical path) is:
questions.solution: Path durations: 1-2-4 = 12, 1-3-4 = 11. The critical path is the longest = 12 days.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Inventory types:
options: Raw materials, WIP, finished goods, MRO (maintenance, repair, operating supplies)
questions.solution: Inventory: decoupling, smoothing, hedge against uncertainty. Carrying cost ~20-30% of item value.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Forecast — seasonal ARIMA (SARIMA):
options: ARIMA(p,d,q)(P,D,Q)_s. s = seasonal period. Multiplicative seasonality. Box-Jenkins
questions.solution: SARIMA: seasonal differencing. Seasonal AR and MA terms. ACF at lag s, 2s, etc.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: LP — multiple optimal solutions:
options: Objective is parallel to binding constraint. Alternative optima
questions.solution: Multiple: non-basic variable with zero reduced cost. All optimal solutions on edge.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Transportation — degeneracy:
options: Less than m+n-1 occupied cells. Resolve by adding ε allocation
questions.solution: Degeneracy: insufficient basic variables. MODI fails. Add epsilon to non-degenerate.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Sensitivity analysis: right-hand side ranging:
options: Range over which current basis remains optimal (allowable increase/decrease in b_i)
questions.solution: RHS range: current shadow price valid within range. Outside: basis changes. Shadow price changes.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Assignment — maximization:
options: Negate costs or use (max - cost) matrix. Hungarian method works
questions.solution: Maximization: convert to minimization by subtracting from maximum.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Simplex method:
options: Iterative algorithm moving from vertex to vertex on feasible region boundary
questions.solution: Simplex: slack/surplus variables. Optimal when all reduced costs ≤ 0 (maximization).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Customers arrive at 10/hour and spend on average 0.75 hour in the system. The average number in the system is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Simulation: types of random variates for interarrival times in M/M/1:
questions.solution: M/M/1: Poisson arrivals (exponential interarrival), exponential service. Generate exponential RVs.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Economic batch quantity (EBQ) for production:
options: Q = sqrt(2DS/H * p/(p-d)) where p=production rate, d=demand rate
questions.solution: EBQ: finite replenishment. Factor p/(p-d) > 1 makes EBQ > EOQ.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: With demand 2000/yr, order cost ₹40 and order quantity 400, the annual ordering cost is:

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Goal programming preemptive method:
options: Optimizes goals sequentially in priority order
questions.solution: Preemptive: strict priority hierarchy. Higher priority satisfied first.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Material handling principle: unit load means:
options: Moving items together in one load
questions.solution: Unit load: combine items into single move. Reduces trips, improves efficiency. Palletization, containerization.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Chaos theory in management suggests:
options: Small changes can have large unpredictable effects
questions.solution: Chaos: sensitive dependence on initial conditions.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Single-period — underage cost Cu:
options: P(shortage) = Cu/(Cu+Co). Profit lost per unit under-ordered
questions.solution: Newsvendor: Cu = marginal profit (p-c). Co = cost - salvage (c-s). Service level = Cu/(Cu+Co).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Reliability bathtub curve — infant mortality:
options: High initial failures. Causes: defects, misassembly. Burn-in test eliminates
questions.solution: Infant mortality: decreasing failure rate. Burn-in: 24-168 hours. Environmental stress screening (ESS).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Customers arrive at 15/hour and spend on average 1 hour in the system. The average number in the system is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: 5S: Sort, Set in order, Shine, Standardize, Sustain:
questions.solution: 5S: Japanese workplace organization. Reduces waste, improves safety, increases efficiency.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Cellular manufacturing:
options: Group Technology (GT). Machines grouped by part family. Reduced setup, WIP
questions.solution: GT: production flow analysis (PFA). Cell: from-to chart. U-shaped cells.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Game theory — Dominance:
options: Remove dominated strategies (worse than another in all outcomes). Simplify the game
questions.solution: Dominance: row i dominates row k if a_ij ≥ a_kj for all j (max). Column: vice versa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For D = 6400/yr, S = ₹80, H = ₹2/unit/yr, the minimum total ordering-plus-holding cost (at EOQ ≈ 715.5) is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Cross-docking — types:
options: Pre-distribution (labeled at source) vs post-distribution (sort at DC). Flow-through rate >30%
questions.solution: Cross-docking: Wal-Mart pioneered. Flow-through: % cross-docked. Requires advanced WMS.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Bootstrap SPC:
options: Nonparametric control limits using bootstrap samples. For non-normal data. No distribution assumption
questions.solution: Bootstrap: resample from data. Replacements. B = 1000 samples. Limits from percentiles (25th/975th).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Economic Order Quantity (EOQ) minimizes the sum of:
questions.solution: EOQ balances annual ordering cost against annual holding cost to minimize total inventory cost.
